title: Lemon tart
subtitle: Pâte sucrée case with a smooth lemon custard
excerpt: A crisp, sweet pastry case filled with a lemon custard of eggs, cream, sugar, and fresh lemon juice. The filling is poured into the blind-baked case and cooked at low heat so the custard sets without curdling. Classic French tarte au citron.
sourceNotes: Tarte au citron is a classic of French pâtisserie, documented in French household cookery guides from the late 19th century. A comparable lemon cream tart appears in older French culinary literature and is documented in detail in period cookbooks that entered the public domain before 1928. The pâte sucrée (sweet pastry) case is the standard French base for citrus tarts; the egg-cream-lemon filling is a French lemon curd variant.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The baked tart keeps refrigerated for 2 days. The pastry softens slightly by day 2; serve the same day or the next for the best texture.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven for the filling bake. Fan at 130°C. The blind bake for the case uses 180°C. Low heat for the filling prevents the eggs from curdling.

glossaryTerms.definition: A correctly baked lemon tart filling should wobble as a set unit when the tin is gently shaken, like a just-set pannacotta. The edges should be fully set and the centre should move slightly without being liquid. It firms further as it cools. If it is fully set in the oven with no wobble, it will be overcooked and grainy once cold.

content.text: A lemon tart is two separate tasks: the pastry case and the filling. Both must succeed. The case is blind-baked until fully crisp before the filling goes in; a soft case will go soggy. The filling is cooked at low heat and the
is the timing cue: set at the edges, slightly mobile in the centre.

Rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Mix in the egg yolk and enough cold milk to bring it to a firm dough. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to 3 mm thick and use to line a 23 cm loose-bottomed tart tin. Press into the sides, trim the overhang, and return to the refrigerator for 15 minutes. Preheat the oven to 180°C conventional.

Line the chilled pastry case with baking paper and fill with baking beans or uncooked rice. Bake for 20 minutes, then remove the paper and beans and bake for a further 8 to 10 minutes until the base is pale gold and fully dry to the touch. Reduce the oven to 150°C.

Whisk together the eggs, egg yolks, and sugar until combined. Add the cream, lemon juice, and zest and whisk until smooth. Place the tart tin on a baking tray and pour the filling directly into the hot case — this minimises the time the filling is in an unbaked case. Bake at 150°C for 25 to 30 minutes until set at the edges with a gentle wobble at the centre. Leave to cool to room temperature in the tin before slicing.
